sessions.lever: remove 3 score-inert cheat-asm scheduling barriers in cases 0/1
sessions.note: Source cleanup only — barriers were verified score-inert. Multiple structural variants of case 2 (== 0 return / !=0 goto / inline done block) and case 4/5/6 (fall-through vs duplicate-and-merge) all produced floor 6. No structural lever found that lowers the floor.

sessions.lever: session 2 — 3 new variants tested at candidate floor; objdump diff cluster pinned exactly
sessions.note: Confirmed candidate floor 6 from a clean apply. Disassembled both objects (build/src/code6cac_c_ab.o canonical vs tmp/sandbox/func_8003AB44/code6cac_c_ab.o stripped) and confirmed exactly 7 diff slots: 4 at byte offsets b8-c4 (case 2 polarity: target beqz/move/j/li vs ours bnez/li/j/move) and 3 at offsets 130/138/13c (case 5/6 register: target $v0, ours $v1). Masked Lev floor: 6. 3 new structural variants rejected (see rejected_forms). scan_hand_coded tier LOW (score 0, no S1-S8 signals) — confirmed pure-C target; canonical-asm authorization is NOT applicable per [[canonical-gate-distance-not-evidence]].

sessions.lever: session 3 — 4 additional structural variants rejected at candidate floor; diff cluster re-confirmed via objdump
sessions.note: Re-confirmed candidate floor 6 from a clean apply (candidate matches session-2 state). Re-disassembled sandbox object and matched exactly the same 7 diff slots (offsets b8-c4 polarity, 130/138/13c register choice) — no progress on either gap. 4 new structural variants tested at candidate floor (see rejected_forms). Sibling matched switches in code6cac_c_ab.c (suDispMentalBar, func_8003AE5C) all use switch-with-break pattern (no early return per case), so their codegen does not transfer to this function's switch-with-early-return shape. Per [[difficult-is-not-impossible]] the remaining gap is genuinely RTL-level reorg.c invert peephole + RA conservative live-set; the manual structural lever space is now near-exhausted. Next-session priorities should shift to instrumented BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG dump on case 5/6 (to learn the actual pseudo number / conflict set) and directed-permuter from candidate.c.

sessions.lever: session 4 — executed BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG diagnostic on isolated func_8003AB44; allocno table confirms single non-trivial allocno; the case 5/6 issue isolated as ord=0 allocation excluding $v0
sessions.note: Applied candidate.c to src/ (floor 6 reconfirmed via sandbox: build_insns=target_insns=93, cheat_asm_stripped=6, rules_dropped=5). Used tmp/gccdbg/cc1 (instrumented build SHA1 a17fc6bbcb... May 30, canonical untouched) to dump BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG on a single-function isolated preprocessed input (tools/decomp-permuter/strip_other_fns.py + cc1 -O2 -G0 -funsigned-char -mcpu=3000 -mips1 -mno-abicalls -fno-builtin). Result: ONE allocno only — `ALLOCDBG ord=0 pseudo=94 hardreg=3 nrefs=3 livelen=6 pri=5000`. Pseudo 94 = the case 5/6 lbu/addiu/sb increment value. hardreg=3 = $v1. Allocated FIRST (ord=0), yet picked $v1 over $v0 — meaning $v0 was EXCLUDED from the available set when this allocno was processed. Since no other allocno claims $v0, the exclusion must come from basic_block_live_at_start[.L8003AC74] (the case 5/6 jtbl-entry block) containing $v0 in its live-in set. This confirms session 3's hypothesis explicitly: flow.c's conservative live-set computation at the multi-entry jtbl block keeps $v0 marked live-in. Target asm at the same offsets (2B474-2B488): `lbu $v0,gprel(D_800A38AC); nop; addiu $v0,$v0,1; sb $v0,gprel(D_800A38AC); j .L8003ACA8; addu $v0,$zero,$zero (delay slot)` — confirms target REUSES $v0 across the increment cluster AND the return-value setup. Why target lands the same pseudo in $v0: target's compilation had $v0 dead at the case 5/6 entry, so default ascending preference picked it. The remaining un-tested lever class: defeat the flow.c $v0 live-in by ensuring every predecessor of .L8003AC74 has $v0 explicitly killed before reaching it (notably the case 4 fall-through path after `jal gpu_SetDispMask(0)` — semantically $v0 IS clobbered by the call but flow.c's call-clobber tracking may be insufficient at the jtbl-merge join). Concrete next-session hypothesis: try a pure-C lever that explicitly kills $v0 along the case 4 path before fall-through, OR splits case 4 with semantic content that prevents jump2 cross-jump from re-merging. Both involve some sort of structural change that hasn't been derived yet.

sessions.lever: session 5 — tested case-order flip variant (case 5/6 before case 4) at candidate base; manual lever space confirmed exhausted
sessions.note: Applied candidate.c, confirmed clean floor 6 (build_insns=target_insns=93, rules_dropped=5). Tested 1 new structural variant beyond session-4's documented rejected_forms: source-order flip — case 5/6 cases physically BEFORE case 4 in source, with duplicate `D_800A38AC++; return 0;` in case 4 (no fall-through). Hypothesis: cc1 emits RTL blocks in source order; reversing case 5/6 vs case 4 might change cc1's RTL block traversal and thus flow.c's basic_block_live_at_start traversal order, possibly affecting whether $v0 is marked live-in at the case 5/6 entry block. Result: score=6, build_insns=93 (no change). cc1's jump2 cross-jump merge still consolidates the duplicate increment blocks; the merge target is determined by cross-jump's choice, and the flow.c live-set computation appears insensitive to source order at this level (allocno priority and live-set rebuild on each compile yields identical pseudo 94 → $v1 result). Confirms session-3+4's diagnosis: the case 5/6 register issue is a deep flow.c property of the jtbl-merge join block, NOT a function of how cases are syntactically ordered. Manual structural lever space now spans 12+ rejected variants across 5 sessions; the case 2 polarity (4 diffs) and case 5/6 register (3 diffs) remaining gap is the deep RTL-level coupling session 3-4 documented. The un-tried lever class is directed-permuter from candidate.c base (PERM_GENERAL type-mutation + statement-reorder); next session should set up permuter/func_8003AB44/ (target.s from prelude+asm/funcs/func_8003AB44.s, base.c from preprocessed candidate, compile.sh from dbe4/marionation template) and run an extended sweep. Cheat-reviewer NOT re-invoked: session 5's candidate is unchanged from session-4's PASS-vetted form (the case-order variant was tested locally but reverted; the saved candidate.c is canonical session-4 form).

reviewer.evidence: Session 4 invoked cheat-reviewer agent on memory/wip/func_8003AB44/candidate.c. Verdict PASS: candidate removes 3 forbidden __asm__ __volatile__("" ::: "memory") scheduling barriers from cases 0/1 and adds zero new constructs. Remaining body is semantically correct, idiomatic state-machine dispatch C that passes all 6 checklist tests (semantic purpose / human-programmer / GCC-internals justification / permuter-derived / family check / naming-announces-intent). The goto done/retry/fail labels are SOTN-accepted mixed exit forms per [[cross-jump-store-tail-merge]]. Reviewer summary: "Candidate removes 3 forbidden __asm__ __volatile__('' ::: 'memory') scheduling barriers from cases 0/1 and adds zero new constructs; the remaining body is semantically correct, idiomatic C that passes all 6 checklist tests."

remaining_gap.summary: 6-distance gap composed of (a) case 2 branch polarity (target wants `beqz $v0, end; move $v0,0 (delay); j done; addiu $a0,3 (delay)`; GCC naturally emits `bnez $v0, done; addiu $a0,3 (delay); j end; move $v0,0 (delay)` — opposite branch sense + delay-slot fill choice); (b) case 5/6 increment register ($v1 in our build, $v0 in target; the lbu/addiu/sb chain on D_800A38AC).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: All 5 existing rules retained: regfix.txt has 4 substs at idx 42-45 (bne->beq + delay slot pair swap + j target swap) covering case 2 polarity, plus 1 $2<->$3 swap @ 72-75 covering case 5/6 register.

next_hypotheses: PRIORITY 1: Set up permuter/func_8003AB44/ workspace and run directed-permuter sweep from candidate.c. Reference setup: permuter/dbe4/ template (compile.sh: cc1 -O2 -G0 -funsigned-char -quiet -mcpu=3000 -mips1 -mno-abicalls -fno-builtin -w | prologue_fix | maspsx --aspsx-version=2.34 | as -march=r3000). target.s = tools/decomp-permuter/prelude.inc (drop `.set gp=64`) + asm/funcs/func_8003AB44.s. base.c = preprocessed src/code6cac_c_ab.c with candidate.c body, then tools/decomp-permuter/strip_other_fns.py to isolate. Run with `--stop-on-zero` and PERM_GENERAL + PERM_INT_TYPE enabled. Base score expectation: ~30 (= 6 reg-diff × 5). Sessions 4-5 confirm manual lever space is exhausted; permuter randomization is the remaining unexplored search direction.

PRIORITY 2 (if permuter exhausted): Instrumented cc1 dump with BB2_FLOW_DEBUG hooks to read `basic_block_live_at_start[.L8003AC74]` directly. The BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG session 4 ran inferred $v0 is in this live-set; a direct readout would confirm AND show WHICH predecessor's live-out propagated it. If it's the jtbl-jr's live-out (not case 4 fall-through), the lever shifts toward changing jtbl-dispatch block live-set, not case 4 path.

PRIORITY 3: Read m2c-reconstructed source for func_8003AB44 (run m2c against asm/funcs/func_8003AB44.s; output may suggest a structural form the hand-decompilation missed). Compare against the candidate.c form for ANY semantic difference, no matter how small.

PRIORITY 4: Cross-reference matched sibling switch-dispatch in code6cac_c_ab.c — examine the LREG/GREG dumps for those siblings to see if any have a 5/6-case-with-fall-through-from-4 shape that allocated $v0 naturally. The sibling's C structure would be the template.

RULED OUT: case-order flip (session 5), shared-end-label restructure (session 1 inline-done variant), explicit constant store in case 4 (would cost +4 insns per session-2 measurement), source-syntactic variants (12+ rejected forms across sessions 1-4).

rejected_forms.form: Inline done block in case 2 (no goto)
rejected_forms.reason: Right polarity but -1 insn count (92 vs 93 target) — cross-jump merge consolidates case 3's done body into case 2's inline block, losing the j done + addiu $a0,3 delay slot pair. Not lower than HEAD's 6.

rejected_forms.form: Swap case 2 test sense (`if (X) goto done; return 0;` vs `if (X == 0) return 0; goto done;`)
rejected_forms.reason: Identical emission — GCC sees same RTL regardless of syntactic flip.

rejected_forms.form: Split case 4 / case 5/6 with duplicate increment in each
rejected_forms.reason: Cross-jump merge consolidated them back; register choice ($v1) unchanged.

rejected_forms.form: Invert case 3 test (`!= 0 goto retry`) with inlined done as fall-through
rejected_forms.reason: Same as inline form; GCC's cross-jump optimization still routes case 3 to case 2's done block.

rejected_forms.form: Case 2 named local for call return: `s32 r; r = func_8008C464(3, 1, 0); if (r == 0) return 0; goto done;`
rejected_forms.reason: GCC sees the local as a single-use alias and folds back to the same RTL pattern. The named local extension from [[call-return-if-result-reuse-v0]] does NOT apply here because the construct is an early-return, not an if-else merge form. r's pseudo lives only across the test; doesn't displace $v0 from the post-call dispatch.

rejected_forms.form: Case 2 `return r` (where r is the captured call return that we know is 0)
rejected_forms.reason: GCC's constant propagation sees `r == 0` in the taken-branch context and folds `return r;` back to `return 0;`. Same RTL as baseline. Cannot defeat the constant fold without an opaque-arithmetic construct (which is a cheat per [[loop-rotation-two-shift]] discussion of forbidden opaque vars in non-loop contexts).

rejected_forms.form: Case 5/6 named local for increment: `{ s32 t = D_800A38AC; D_800A38AC = t + 1; return 0; }`
rejected_forms.reason: GCC sees through the trivial alias — emits the same lbu/addiu/sb sequence. Register choice remains $v1. The named-local-bias works for call-return-flow ([[call-return-if-result-reuse-v0]]) but a load-then-store of a global is opaque to RA's live-range analysis here; the pseudo's life is fully contained within the 3-insn RMW, no way to extend it without semantic change.

rejected_forms.form: Case 2 else-if cascade: `if (X & 0x10) goto fail; else if (call != 0) goto done; return 0;`
rejected_forms.reason: Syntactic — GCC's RTL expand collapses the else-if to the same RTL pattern as the bare `if (X & 0x10) goto fail; if (call == 0) return 0; goto done;`. No polarity shift, no priority shift. The semicolon position is invisible to the optimizer.

rejected_forms.form: Case 2 fully inline done body in case 2 (rejecting the goto): `if (X & 0x10) goto fail; if (call != 0) { call2; D_800A38AC = 4; } return 0;`
rejected_forms.reason: Re-tested 2026-06-10 session 3 to confirm session-1's measurement: build_insns=92 (target=93), polarity flips correctly but cross-jump-merge in jump2 consolidates case 3's done body into case 2's inline, dropping the `j done; addiu $a0,3` pair. Same outcome as rejected_forms[0]. Confirms cross-jump-merge cannot be defeated by inlining at the source level when both targets have the same body.

rejected_forms.form: Collapse case 4/5/6 into one block with internal if-check: `case 4: case 5: case 6: if (D_800A38AC == 4) gpu_SetDispMask(0); D_800A38AC++; return 0;`
rejected_forms.reason: build_insns 97 (vs target 93): the `if (D_800A38AC == 4)` adds an lbu/li/bne dispatch INSIDE the merged body, costing 4 net instructions. Semantically equivalent but structurally over-eager — collapsing the fall-through cases obscures the original switch shape and forces an extra dispatch GCC otherwise gets for free from the jtbl.

rejected_forms.form: Goto-merge in case 4/5/6: `case 4: gpu_SetDispMask(0); goto bump; case 5: case 6: bump: D_800A38AC++; return 0;`
rejected_forms.reason: Compiles to identical RTL as the case 4 fall-through form — GCC sees the goto-to-immediately-following-label as a no-op control transfer and folds it. No change in case 5/6 register choice ($v1 still); same lbu/addiu/sb cluster.

rejected_forms.form: Source-order flip: `case 5: case 6: D_800A38AC++; return 0; case 4: gpu_SetDispMask(0); D_800A38AC++; return 0;` (case 5/6 BEFORE case 4 in source, with duplicate increment in case 4)
rejected_forms.reason: Session 5 (2026-06-10). cc1's jump2 cross-jump-merge consolidates the duplicate `D_800A38AC++; return 0;` blocks; merge target's predecessor structure is the same regardless of source order (case 4 fall-through + 2 jtbl entries). flow.c live-set computation at the jtbl-merge block is insensitive to syntactic case order — pseudo 94 still allocates to $v1, build_insns=93. Confirms source-order is NOT a lever; the case 5/6 register issue is a deep property of the predecessor live-out / multi-entry jtbl join, not a function of cc1's RTL block emission order.
